pattern: Four rolling state variables — best balance after the first buy, first sell, second buy, and second sell — let two non-overlapping transactions be optimized in a single linear sweep.
hints: You may complete at most two trades and never hold two positions at once, so think about the order: buy, sell, buy, sell.
Track the best running value of each of the four stages, where a later stage builds on the profit locked in by the earlier one.
Maintain buy1 = max(buy1, -p), sell1 = max(sell1, buy1 + p), buy2 = max(buy2, sell1 - p), sell2 = max(sell2, buy2 + p); the answer is sell2.

edge_inputs.description: one long rise is best taken as a single trade
classic two-peak shape splits into two trades
min then max in two days

stress.description: long random price series exercising the four-state DP

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxProfit(self, prices):
        buy1 = float('-inf')
        sell1 = 0
        buy2 = float('-inf')
        sell2 = 0
        for p in prices:
            buy1 = max(buy1, -p)
            sell1 = max(sell1, buy1 + p)
            buy2 = max(buy2, sell1 - p)
            sell2 = max(sell2, buy2 + p)
        return sell2
